revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Butsuzōzui]][[ja:仏像図彙]][[zh:佛像圖彙]]


{{Short description|Collection of Buddhist iconographic sketches|qq=}}
{{Italic title|reason=|qq=}}
The {{nihongo|'''''Butsuzōzui'''''|仏像図彙||qq=}} ("Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images") is a collection of [[:en:Buddhist iconography|Buddhist iconographic]] sketches said to have been painted by {{Nihongo|Hidenobu Tosa|土佐秀信|Tosa, Hidenobu|qq=}} of the {{ill|Tosa school|en|Tosa school|qid=Q2915215|lt=Tosa school|1=Tosa school}}. Originally published in 1690 (''{{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}}'' 3) in five volumes, it comprises more than 800 sketches, inspired by the Chinese style of paintings called Paihuo, with the Buddhist icons divided into five parts and further categorized. In {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo-period|1=Edo period}} Japan the Butsuzōzui compendium was the most widely distributed source for information on {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|qid=Q736311|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism in Japan}} and [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu]] deities.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Fowler|first1=Sherry|title=Japan-Studies.org/Butsuzōzui.html|url=http://www.japan-studies.org/Butsuz%C5%8Dzui.html|access-date=2016-12-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161220184833/http://www.japan-studies.org/Butsuz%C5%8Dzui.html|archive-date=2016-12-20|url-status=dead|qq=}}</ref>
Included area of interest ranges from {{ill|Nyorai|en|Nyorai|qid=Q17052684|lt=Nyorai|1=Nyorai}} and {{ill|Bosatsu|en|Bosatsu|qid=Q23925162|lt=Bosatsu|1=Bosatsu}} as well as folk deities including {{Nihongo|2=鬼神|3=Kijin|qq=}}, {{Nihongo|2=暦神|3=Rekijin|qq=}} and {{Nihongo|2=習合神|3=Shūgōshin|qq=}} as well as fixtures and {{Nihongo|tools and fixtures applied to rituals|仏具、祭器|[[:ja:仏具|Butsugiu]], [[:ja:祭器|saiki]]|qq=}}. However criticized for errors and misunderstanding, a revised and expanded edition, "{{Nihongo|2=増補諸宗 仏像図彙|3=Zōho shoshū butsuzō zui|qq=}}"  was issued in 1792 (''{{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}}'' 4), a person in Ōsaka, {{Nihongo|2=土佐将曹紀秀信|3=Tosa shōsō Ki no Hidenobu|qq=}} . The note to the first edition tells that it was edited by {{Nihongo|2=[[:ja:指月軒義山|指月軒義山]]|3=Shigetsuken Gizan|qq=}}.
[[File:Butsuzo zui.jpg|thumb|120px|Butsuzōzui]]
==See also==
*[[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikokunyo (goddess)]]
*[[Kisshōten|Kichijōten (goddess)]]
== Further reading ==
* ''{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|qid=Q5698601|lt=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|1=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia}}'' (1935), volume 22. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* Tosa, Hidenobu (1982) ''Zōho shoshū Butsuzōzui'', "Von Siebold collection", {{NCID|BA58520258|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* Tosa, Hidenobu (1998) ''Butsuzōzui, Zōho Butsuzōzui'', Ōzorasha, in "{{Nihongo|2=訓蒙図彙集成|3=Kummōzui Shūsei|4=Series|qq=}}" volume 14, {{ISBN|4756805175|qq=}}, {{NCID|BA3883839X|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* Togo, Fukiko "Two Kinds of Hindu and Buddhist Images in Nepal" ''Sonoda journal'', {{ISSN|0286-2816|qq=}}, Sonoda Women's University, March 1986, Issue 20, pp67–86. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* {{nihongo|1=Umehara, Tatsuji|2=梅原達治|qq=}} "{{nihongo|2=民間信仰としての社日祭祀の形成|3=Minkan shinkō to shite no Shujitsu saiki no Keisei|qq=}}, ''The Journal of the Faculty of General Education'', Sapporo University, {{ISSN|0288-2221|qq=}}, March 1988, issue 32, pp.107-144. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* Emoto, Hiroshi "Inuhariko, with Notes and Discussion" ''Otsuma Women's University, annual report. Humanities and Social Sciences'', {{ISSN|0916-7692|qq=}}, Otsuma Women's University, March 2000, issue 32, pp. 71–97. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* MIYAZAKI, Katsunori "The Bibliography Research of Siebold 'NIPPON' : The Watermark of 'NIPPON' and Distribution" ''{{nihongo|1=Kyūshū University Museum Report|2=九州大学総合研究博物館研究報告|qq=}}'', {{ISSN|1348-3080|qq=}}, Kyushu University, April 2004, volume 2, pp. 1–32{{doi|10.15017/7980|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* MIYAZAKI, Katsunori "The restoration of Siebold 'NIPPON" ''Kyūshū University Museum Report'', {{ISSN|1348-3080|qq=}}, Kyushu University, March 2005 volume 3, pp. 23–105, {{doi|10.15017/7984|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* YORITOMI, Motohiro "Unification of Buddhism and Shinto through the Mahabhijnajnanabhibhu-buddha" ''Bulletin of Buddhist Cultural Institute, Ryukoku University'', {{ISSN|0289-5544|qq=}}, Ryukoku University, 30 November 2006, issue 45, p. 143. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* MIYAZAKI, Katsunori "The Study of Comparing Color Prints in Siebold 'NIPPON'"	''Kyūshū University Museum Report'', {{ISSN|1348-3080|qq=}}, Kyushu University, January 2007, volume 5, pp. 1–56, {{doi|10.15017/10285|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* KIKUFUJI, Akimichi "The Preaching of Sojun, the Compiler of the Myokonin-den", ''{{nihongo|1=Journal of Indian and Buddhist Studies|3=Indogaku Bukkyogaku Kenkyu|qq=}}'', {{ISSN|0019-4344|qq=}}, Japanese Association of Indian and Buddhist Studies, 2009, volume 58, issue 1, pp. 12–19, {{doi|10.4259/ibk.58.1_12|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
* MOCHIZUKI, Shincho "The Lotus Faith of a Wealthy Merchant Chaya Family", ''Journal of Indian and Buddhist Studies (Indogaku Bukkyogaku Kenkyu)'', {{ISSN|0019-4344|qq=}}, Japanese Association of Indian and Buddhist Studies, 2014, volume 63, issue 1, pp. 189–196, {{doi|10.4259/ibk.63.1_189|qq=}}. {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
==References==
{{reflist|qq=}}
==External links==
*[http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html Online photographic copy of the Butsuzōzui compendium (1783 edition) (Ehime University Library)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html |date=2018-10-10 |qq=}} {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
*{{nihongo||仏像図彙|[{{NDLDC|3442141|qq=}} Butsuzō-zui]|qq=}}, {{in lang|ja|qq=}}, volume 1, reprint (1783 edition), drawn by Hidenobu Tosa, published by Den’eon Takeda, 1900 (''{{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}}'' 33), {{JPNO|68012533|qq=}} {{doi|10.11501/3442141|qq=}}. National Diet Library of Japan. Open Access.
*{{nihongo||諸宗仏像図彙|[{{NDLDC|818698|qq=}} Shoshū butsuzō-zui]|qq=}}, {{in lang|ja|qq=}}, volume １. Handcopied version of the revised "Butsuzō zui", Kajikawa Shinji, Kyoto : Shinji Kajikawa (manual transcription) ; 1886 (''Meiji '' 19). {{JPNO|40045652|qq=}}, {{doi|10.11501/818698|qq=}}. National Diet Library of Japan. Open access.
